events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Scenario: aluminum conductor on device not rated CO/ALR. This is a known hazard: aluminum conductors must terminate on devices listed and marked CO/ALR (or AL-CU) for branch circuit wiring; otherwise risk of overheating/fire. Work rough-complete. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Aluminum on non-CO/ALR device is fire hazard, critical maybe. But typical risk high. Need decide. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Findings: aluminum conductor terminated on device terminal not rated CO/ALR; incompatible material causing oxidation/loosening, overheating. Actions: replace device with CO/ALR-rated device or use approved connector/pigtail, verify torque, inspect all aluminum terminations. Rationale: NEC requires terminals for aluminum conductors be listed and identified for such use; CO/ALR marking required for outlets/switches. References: NEC 110.3(B), 110.14, 406.2(C), maybe 310.16 for ampacity but not central. Need include NEC 310 and Table 310.16 as references per instruction. Also maybe 110.14. Use value? Not asked numeric reading. Could set 0. percent_complete maybe 80. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source "", order [], workable [].

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response

  "findings": [
    "Aluminum conductor is terminated on a device terminal that is not marked CO/ALR.",
    "Incompatible aluminum-to-device termination can loosen and overheat due to oxidation and differential expansion.",
    "This is a fire and equipment damage hazard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the non-rated device and replace it with a device listed and marked CO/ALR for aluminum conductors.",
    "Verify all aluminum terminations in the circuit are on CO/ALR-rated devices or use approved connectors/pigtails.",
    "Torque terminations to manufacturer specifications and verify with inspection."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC requires terminals and devices used with aluminum conductors to be listed and identified for such use. A device without a CO/ALR marking is not suitable for direct termination of aluminum branch-circuit conductors, creating an overheating/fire hazard.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC 406.2(C)",
    "NEC 310",
    "NEC Table 310.16"
  ]
}
